title: Callista ovata
intro: Callista ovata, also known as the red bead tree or coral tree, is a plant species that belongs to the Fabaceae family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and alleviating pain. The bioactive constituents of Callista ovata include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Callista ovata is commonly used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are often used to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, and to aid in digestive health.
taxonomy: Callista ovata is a species of sea snail, specifically a marine gastropod mollusk. It belongs to the family Veneridae, which includes pearl mussels and other related species. Within the family Veneridae, Callista ovata is classified under the subfamily Venerinae. This classification reflects the snail's unique characteristics and its evolutionary relationships with other species in the family. By grouping Callista ovata within Venerinae, scientists can better understand its place within the larger taxonomic hierarchy of mollusks.
